message:
fix(ci): post-release patch for Homebrew cask caveats $() breakage

GoReleaser's cask template renders caveats as double-quoted Ruby
strings, which breaks $() subshells (eval "$(ntm init ...)"). This
has regressed at v1.7.0 and v1.8.0 after manual fixes.

Add an automated post-release step that checks out the homebrew-tap
repo and rewrites the caveats block to use heredoc syntax (<<~EOS),
preventing the regression from recurring on future releases.

Fixes #91

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -73,6 +73,94 @@ jobs:
           HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
           SCOOP_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SCOOP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
 
+      # ----------------------------------------------------------------
+      # Patch Homebrew cask caveats (workaround for goreleaser #5211)
+      # GoReleaser renders caveats as double-quoted Ruby strings, which
+      # breaks $() subshells. Replace with heredoc syntax (<<~EOS).
+      # See: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm/issues/91
+      # ----------------------------------------------------------------
+      - name: Patch Homebrew cask caveats
+        env:
+          HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN }}
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+
+          CASK="Casks/ntm.rb"
+          REPO_URL="https://x-access-token:${HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/Dicklesworthstone/homebrew-tap.git"
+
+          git clone --depth 1 "$REPO_URL" homebrew-tap-patch
+          cd homebrew-tap-patch
+
+          if [ ! -f "$CASK" ]; then
+            echo "::warning::$CASK not found in homebrew-tap — skipping caveats patch"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          # Check whether the cask already uses heredoc caveats (idempotent)
+          if grep -q '<<~EOS' "$CASK"; then
+            echo "Cask already uses heredoc caveats — nothing to patch"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          # Build the replacement caveats block using heredoc syntax
+          python3 - "$CASK" <<'PYEOF'
+          import sys, re
+
+          path = sys.argv[1]
+          text = open(path).read()
+
+          # Match the entire caveats block: "caveats do\n ... \n  end"
+          pattern = re.compile(
+              r'(?P<indent>[ \t]*)caveats\s+do\n'   # opening line
+              r'(?P<body>.*?)\n'                      # body (non-greedy)
+              r'(?P=indent)end',                      # closing end
+              re.DOTALL,
+          )
+
+          m = pattern.search(text)
+          if not m:
+              print("::warning::Could not locate caveats block — skipping patch")
+              sys.exit(0)
+
+          indent = m.group("indent")
+          inner = indent + "  "
+
+          replacement = (
+              f'{indent}caveats <<~EOS\n'
+              f'{inner}To enable shell integration, add to your shell rc file:\n'
+              f'\n'
+              f'{inner}  eval "$(ntm init zsh)"   # for zsh\n'
+              f'{inner}  eval "$(ntm init bash)"  # for bash\n'
+              f'{inner}  eval "$(ntm init fish)"  # for fish\n'
+              f'\n'
+              f'{inner}Run \'ntm tutorial\' to get started!\n'
+              f'{indent}EOS'
+          )
+
+          patched = text[:m.start()] + replacement + text[m.end():]
+          open(path, "w").write(patched)
+          print("Patched caveats block to use heredoc syntax")
+          PYEOF
+
+          # Verify the patch didn't break Ruby syntax
+          if command -v ruby >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+            ruby -c "$CASK" || { echo "::error::Ruby syntax check failed after patching"; exit 1; }
+          fi
+
+          if git diff --quiet "$CASK"; then
+            echo "No changes after patch — cask was already correct"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          git config user.name  "github-actions[bot]"
+          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
+          git add "$CASK"
+          git commit -m "fix(cask): use heredoc caveats to avoid Ruby \$() breakage
+
+          Automated post-release patch — see Dicklesworthstone/ntm#91"
+          git push
+          echo "Pushed caveats fix to homebrew-tap"
+
       - name: Upload Release Artifacts
         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
         with:
